title: Kyiv
content: Kyiv is the capital and largest city of Ukraine, set on the Dnieper River. Landmarks include Saint Sophia Cathedral and the Kyiv Pechersk Lavra cave monastery. It is the country's main cultural, financial, and technology hub, with a fast-growing startup scene.

title: Lviv
content: Lviv is a city in western Ukraine known for its well-preserved historic centre, a UNESCO World Heritage site. It has a strong coffee-house culture, many churches blending architectural styles, and a lively IT industry.

title: Odesa
content: Odesa is a port city on Ukraine's Black Sea coast. It is famous for the Potemkin Stairs, a warm-season beach scene, and a historic multicultural old town. The port makes it a key hub for maritime trade and grain export.

title: Warsaw
content: Warsaw is the capital and largest city of Poland, on the Vistula River. Its Old Town was meticulously rebuilt after World War II and is a UNESCO site. The city is Poland's business and financial centre with a dense cluster of modern skyscrapers.

title: Kraków
content: Kraków is a city in southern Poland on the Vistula River, long the country's royal capital. Wawel Castle and the large medieval market square anchor a vast, well-preserved old town. Nearby is the Wieliczka Salt Mine.

title: London
content: London is the capital of the United Kingdom, on the River Thames. It is a leading global financial centre and home to landmarks such as the Tower of London, the British Museum, and a dense network of parks and rail links.

title: Berlin
content: Berlin is the capital of Germany, on the Spree River. Known for the Brandenburg Gate, remnants of the Berlin Wall, and a large museum island, it has a thriving arts and startup culture and extensive green spaces.

title: Gdańsk
content: Gdańsk is a major port city on Poland's Baltic Sea coast, historically a leading Hanseatic trading hub. Its Long Market and brick Gothic architecture draw visitors, and the shipyards are tied to the Solidarity movement.

title: Saint Petersburg
content: Saint Petersburg is a major port city on the Baltic Sea, at the head of the Gulf of Finland on the Neva River. It is one of the largest cities on the Baltic, known for the Hermitage Museum, canals, and imperial palaces.

title: Stockholm
content: Stockholm is the capital and largest city of Sweden, a Baltic Sea port spread across fourteen islands where Lake Mälaren meets the sea. Its preserved old town Gamla Stan and strong tech sector make it a regional hub.

title: Riga
content: Riga is the capital and largest city of Latvia, a Baltic Sea port at the mouth of the Daugava River. It is the biggest city in the Baltic states, famous for its Art Nouveau architecture and a UNESCO-listed historic centre.

title: Helsinki
content: Helsinki is the capital and largest city of Finland, a Baltic Sea port on the Gulf of Finland. Known for neoclassical and functionalist architecture, a vibrant design scene, and busy ferry links across the Baltic.

title: Tallinn
content: Tallinn is the capital and largest city of Estonia, a Baltic Sea port on the Gulf of Finland. Its remarkably intact medieval old town is a UNESCO site, and the city is a noted digital-government and startup hub.

title: Gdynia
content: Gdynia is a port city on Poland's Baltic Sea coast, part of the Tricity metropolitan area with Gdańsk and Sopot. Built largely in the 20th century, it has a modernist centre and one of Poland's main seaports.

title: Szczecin
content: Szczecin is a port city in north-western Poland near the Baltic Sea, on the Oder River close to the German border. It is one of Poland's largest seaports, with broad boulevards laid out in a star pattern.
